data.title: NexLawn introduces 3 new robotic mowers at IFA 2025: VIDAR Series, VIDAR AWD Series, Master X Vison.
data.description: In Berlin on September 5, 2025, NexLawn, a top-tier brand part of MOVA, introduced its revolutionary garden tools and robotic solutions to the world at IFA 2025. The debut included three cutting-edge robotic yard care products: the VIDAR Series, the VIDAR AWD Series, and the innovative Master X Vision.

data.title: Bavarian Nordic A/S sees capital increase of DKK 3,818,710 due to employee warrant exercise.
data.description: Bavarian Nordic A/S (OMX: BAVA) disclosed on September 5, 2025, in Copenhagen, Denmark, that its share capital has surged by DKK 3,818,710 following employees' exercise of warrants.

data.title: Top Wall Street forecasters adjust ABM Industries' expectations for Q3 earnings. Analysts predict earnings at 95 cents per share, up from 94 cents last year, with revenue expected to reach $2.15 billion, compared to $2.09 billion previously. ABM shares closed at $48.10, dropping by 1.3%. Strategic partnership with Nashville Yards cited for expansion in urban facility services.
data.description: ABM Industries Incorporated (NYSE: ABM) is set to announce its third-quarter earnings on Friday, Sept. 5, before the market opens. Analysts are predicting the New York-based company to report earnings of 95 cents per share for the quarter, an increase from the 94 cents per share reported in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be $2.15 billion, up from $2.09 billion in the previous year. ABM recently formed a partnership with Nashville Yards to expand its urban facility services. On Thursday, ABM's stock closed at $48.10, experiencing a 1.3% decrease. For more details and the latest analyst ratings, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Rigaku, Kyoto University, and JEOL form RIGAKU/JEOL-iCeMS Innovation Core for advanced nano crystal electron diffraction analysis.
data.description: On September 4, 2025, Rigaku Corporation in Tokyo, along with Kyoto University and JEOL Ltd., signed an agreement to establish an open innovation organization called RIGAKU/JEOL-iCeMS Innovation Core. This new entity, based in Kyoto University's Institute for Integrated Cell-Material Sciences (iCeMS) analysis center, aims to leverage their strengths for joint research and talent development. The focus will be on innovative molecular structure analysis methods, particularly electron diffraction of nanocrystals. The collaborative research facility will offer advanced testing capabilities to accelerate exploration of new material functionalities, using XtaLAB technology. Rigaku Holdings Corporation spearheads Rigaku, with Jun Kawakami as President and CEO, while Kyoto University is led by President Nagahiro Minato, and JEOL is headed by Izumi Oi.
